author: @CGrenfeldt
text:
I know nothing about 3D and i might buy a printer. It's Interesting you rotated the print. I would have thought it would have been better with 0°.
 I probably missed it, but it would be nice to explain next time [why] you started to rotate it.
It feels like supports wouldn't be necessary if it's not rotated.

author: @stuffoflardohfortheloveof
text: Hi Rob, thanks for this. As you’ve got me interested, I’ve been looking around for other vids on that printer and I now have a little more knowledge but have now one big query (probably more than one but let me start somewhere 🙄)……I get the logic about supports and why it’s important to consider printing at an angle (sometimes) BUT (and forgive me if I’ve missed something obvious) in respect to your turbine blade print why wouldn’t it be simpler to print it 1. At no angle? (ie flat) as this would provide its own raft base (albeit with a few holes) and 2. By doing so it wouldn’t need any supports as the turbine blades would be supporting themselves……(remembering that it’s printing upside down)? Anyone?

author: @kanapki4368
text: You should get a sunlu filament printer on sale then you can print those huge turbine blades you see on thingiverse with a 1mm nozzle fast

author: @kanapki4368
text: Sounds like cura except it looks different

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Oh Wow!! Amazing stuff,,,
And, at least here's the kind of stuff that my Hubby will (Gleefully!) understand Waaaaaay better than I ever possibly will do!!
(*Yeah, Thank you so much Robert, I hope you are Happy about this! Lol!)
Quick question though, has my Hubby been in touch with you?! 
I ask you this, because, he's dancing about my bedsit, singing "Ner NeNe Ner Nah!" & Pointing at me, and asking me "Who's Thick NOW, Eh?!!"
Admittedly, I am not great with wrecknology, having seen my younger brother's "Atari" and 1st home PCs, I thought they were just an Expensive, passing faze!!
(* I'm 99%Right about Most things, but, when I am Wrong, it is usually Spectacularly Wrong!)
I'm glad you are having fun with this, btw, and Hubby, at least isn't wearing his underwear on his head, whilst doing his "Happy Gloating Dance!"
Yay F*cking Computer Printering Wassitsnames!! 
🙂Andrea and Critter Family. XxX

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Excellent video Rob. I really think that resin printers are the future. Again I hope one day you can thread thin copper wires into your projects to do away with messy wiring.
Btw. I always thought that a chitebox was an emergency portable loo. 🤣😂🤣😂
